methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Chips & Semiconductors (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 14 same-sector peers exist in Chips & Semiconductors, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Ranovus is a Series D+ stage semiconductor company that has raised $236 million in total capital, including a $36 million Strategic Innovation Fund grant in August 2025. The broader Chips & Semiconductors sector contains 14 peer companies across public and private markets: 3 public comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private peers including Cerebras, PsiQuantum, Etched, SambaNova Systems, and Credo Semiconductor.

Two standard valuation methods could not be applied to this analysis. Peer-Median benchmarking was inapplicable because the narrow peer set—filtering for same stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier—yielded fewer than three datapoints necessary for statistical reliability, despite the existence of 14 same-sector peers overall. Public-Comp Anchoring was similarly not viable: while the Semiconductors sector shows a median price-to-sales multiple of 24.4x across the three public comparables, Ranovus's revenue is not disclosed, as is typical for private companies, preventing application of this multiple.

This analysis cannot produce a triangulated valuation range without additional data on Ranovus's revenue or comparable transaction multiples within the identified peer universe. A credible range would require either disclosure of the company's top-line metrics or access to recent funding round valuations from the identified private peers. Key uncertainties remain around the company's current revenue scale, growth trajectory, and profitability profile relative to public and private comparables in the sector.
